capability_profile.notes: Side-view, normal gravity. Agent can only move by forces and push the rock via contact.

common_failures: Do not lower minimum_acceptance_tier for simple known tasks just to pass validation.
Keep capability_profile controls honest; no teleporting or direct object mutation.
Tier 5 patterns must be physically solved by check_objective(), not just progress-verified.
generation_guidance: Reuse this objective/capability profile shape when the prompt semantics match.
Do not use validator controls outside capability_profile['allowed_controls'].
Expose progress metrics: agent_rock_distance, rock_distance_to_edge_x, rock_speed, rock_downward_velocity, rock_has_crossed_edge, rock_fall_distance_since_edge_cross, time_remaining.

objective_profile.objective_description: Push the rock over the cliff edge so it leaves support and free-falls into open air.
objective_profile.success_predicate: rock crosses cliff_edge_boundary (support exit) and then falls at least min_fall_distance with downward velocity below -min_downward_velocity

source_prompt: Agent pushes rock off of really tall snowy mountain
summary: Push the rock over the cliff edge so it leaves support and free-falls into open air. Validated as solved_verified with movement=ground_force, gravity=normal.

physics_relations.intent_summary: A controllable agent must physically push a rock so it slides/rolls down from a very tall snowy mountain and leaves the summit area.

relations.repair: Place the agent behind the object relative to the exit boundary and tune mass/friction so contact starts motion.
The boundary must align with the true end of support; support terrain should not be treated as a blocker before the edge.
Keep open air below the edge clear and let gravity produce visible downward displacement after crossing.

validator_strategy.repair_unit: failed relation, not failed task template
validator_strategy.template_policy: helpers are optional constructors; relations are the conceptual source of truth

world_context.movement_model: agent uses ground locomotion with traction that is reduced on ice/snow; can push dynamic bodies via contact forces
world_context.rationale: The prompt centers on pushing and gravity-driven descent; steep snowy terrain plus low friction ensures the rock can be pushed then carried downhill visibly.
world_context.support_model: static terrain made of connected segments/polylines forming a tall mountain with a summit shelf and a long steep descent; world boundaries prevent leaving screen except for designated fall/off-mountain region
world_context.theme: snowy mountain peak with steep downhill slope and icy patches

verification.reason: Code-level objective satisfied during post-subgoal grace validation.
